def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Parameters
    short_ma = params['short_ma']
    long_ma = params['long_ma']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    
    # Calculate indicators
    df['short_ma'] = df['close'].rolling(window=short_ma).mean()
    df['long_ma'] = df['close'].rolling(window=long_ma).mean()
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=14)
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=14).average_true_range()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = np.where(
        (df['short_ma'] > df['long_ma']) & (df['rsi'] < rsi_threshold) & (df['close'] < df['short_ma'] - atr_multiplier * df['atr']),
        1,
        np.where(
            (df['short_ma'] < df['long_ma']) & (df['rsi'] > 100 - rsi_threshold) & (df['close'] > df['short_ma'] + atr_multiplier * df['atr']),
            -1,
            0
        )
    )
    
    return pd.Series(signals, index=df.index).astype(int)
rationale: USD/JPY exhibits mean reversion behavior where deviations from moving averages, confirmed by RSI extremes and ATR-filtered thresholds, signal potential reversals.
